stories.title: Iran's foreign minister leaves Pakistan, then Trump cancels U.S. delegation's travel
stories.description: After arriving on Friday, Iran's Abbas Araghchi has left Islamabad, prompting President Trump to announce that his envoys Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushner will no longer travel there Saturday for peace talks

stories.title: Vessels seized by the U.S. this week were part of a global 'shadow fleet'
stories.description: NPR's Juana Summers speaks with Elisabeth Braw, senior fellow at the Atlantic Council, about shadow vessels, after the U.S. military's seizure of two Iranian-linked oil tankers.

stories.title: Kushner and U.S. envoy Witkoff will head to Pakistan for new Iran talks
stories.description: Iran's foreign minister arrived in Islamabad, and the White House says Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushner will go there Saturday to try to "move the ball forward towards a deal."

stories.title: Israel and Lebanon extend ceasefire. And, Trump eases medical marijuana rules
stories.description: Israel and Lebanon have agreed to extend their ceasefire for three weeks, President Trump says. And, the Trump administration is easing rules on medical marijuana.

stories.title: Thousands of seafarers stranded by ongoing U.S. blockade on Strait of Hormuz
stories.description: As the U.S. blockade on Iranian ports drags on, thousands of seafarers are stranded on ships, and economic shockwaves ripple around the world.

stories.title: Trump cancels US envoys' trip to Pakistan for talks on Iran war
stories.description: Iran had earlier said there were no plans for a direct meeting with a US delegation led by Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushner.

stories.title: Couple discovers Lebanon home destroyed by Israel from satellite image
stories.description: Joe Elias contacted BBC Verify following reports that his village was among several destroyed by Israeli forces.

stories.title: US-Kuwaiti journalist held in Kuwait over social media posts acquitted, lawyers say
stories.description: Ahmed Shihab-Eldin reportedly faced charges, including spreading false information, after he re-posted images related to the Iran war.

stories.title: Lebanon accuses Israel of targeting journalist killed in air strike
stories.description: Lebanon's prime minister accused Israel of war crimes after IDF attacks on Red Cross vehicles also stopped rescuers from reaching the site.

stories.title: Iran says Strait of Hormuz cannot be opened due to ceasefire breaches
stories.description: Iran's chief negotiator says "violations" by the US and Israel make it impossible to open the strait.

stories.title: US and Iran in blockade standoff as Pakistan pushes for talks
stories.description: The mood in the Strait of Hormuz remains combustible despite Trump's ceasefire extension.

stories.title: Tracking ships under fire in the Strait of Hormuz
stories.description: Iran's top negotiator says it's "not possible to reopen" the strait due to "blatent violations" of US-Iran ceasefire.

stories.title: US and Iran 'locked in war of words' says Lyse Doucet
stories.description: The US confirmed on Tuesday night that the Vice-President's trip to Pakistan for a second round of peace talks has been cancelled.

stories.title: Iran says it has seized two ships in Strait of Hormuz after vessels attacked
stories.description: One of the cargo ships was 'heavily damaged' by Iranian forces, a British maritime agency reported on Wednesday.

stories.title: Trump buys time for Iran deal after frantic day of diplomacy
stories.description: The US president's decision marked the second time in as many weeks he has backed off a threat to escalate the war, buying more time.

stories.title: Strait talks: What's at stake in Hormuz
stories.description: The Strait of Hormuz, which is a key passage for oil produced in the Middle East, has come to the center of the Iran-US conflict.

stories.title: A difficult balance to find: Pakistan wants to be a key actor in shaping US-Iran peace
stories.description: Since the beginning of the Iran-US war, Pakistan has tried to host talks between the two countries, aiming at finding peace.

stories.title: Trumps says he is no longer sending envoys to Pakistan for Iran talks
Iran rules out talks with US unless blockade of ports lifted
stories.description: US envoys were expected in the Pakistani capital on Saturday to kickstart a new round of peace negotiations with Iran, although Iranian state media said Tehran's representatives had no immediate plans to hold face-to-face talks. "According to Tehran, until and unless that blockad...

stories.title: Displaced Lebanese remain skeptical despite extended truce
stories.description: In the coastal city of Sidon, displaced families sheltering from months of conflict express little relief at the extension of a fragile truce between Lebanon and Israel. While Donald Trump voiced optimism about a path toward peace, many civilians – uprooted by fighting involving ...

stories.title: US envoys to travel to Islamabad despite Iran ruling out direct negotiations with US
stories.description: US envoys are expected to travel to Pakistan on Saturday in a new bid to salvage ceasefire talks with Tehran, even as Iran ruled out direct negotiations with US representatives as its top diplomat arrived in Islamabad. The latest effort to broker a deal comes as an indefinite cea...

stories.title: Spain PM brushes of reported Pentagon email on punishing NATO members
stories.description: Spanish Prime Minister Pedro Sanchez on Friday refused to be drawn into a dispute with the United States over reports that the Pentagon is weighing whether to punish members of NATO that fail to support American operations in the Iran war. Among those in the firing line is Spain,...

stories.title: Iran war live: Tehran rejects talks under siege, Trump cancels envoys’ trip
stories.description: Trump says Iran did not make a satisfactory offer, as Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi departs Pakistan.

stories.title: How serious is the rift in NATO?
stories.description: Trump fury over Europeans' refusal to join Iran war.

stories.title: Trump puts onus on Iran’s authorities as they project hardened stance
stories.description: Some officials say Tehran must not even entertain any more talks on the nuclear issue amid the threats and blockade.

stories.title: Trump cancels US envoys’ trip after Iran’s Araghchi leaves Pakistan
stories.description: Iranian state media reports foreign minister expected to return to Islamabad on Sunday night.

stories.title: Iran FM Abbas Araghchi and Pakistan PM Shehbaz Sharif meet in Pakistan
stories.description: Iranian FM Araghchi met Pakistani PM Sharif in Islamabad as part of efforts to revive direct negotiations with the US.

stories.title: African governments need to take urgent action on fertiliser shortages
stories.description: Tensions in the Strait of Hormuz have slowed fertiliser shipments which could put harvests at risk.

stories.title: Israel kills four in Lebanon as it escalates ceasefire breaches
stories.description: Raids on a truck and a motorcycle in the town of Yohmor al-Shaqif kill four people, state media report.

stories.title: Indonesian UN peacekeeper becomes sixth killed in Lebanon
stories.description: An Indonesian soldier has become the sixth UN peacekeeper killed in recent hostilities in southern Lebanon.

stories.title: US State Department orders global warning about alleged AI theft by DeepSeek, other Chinese firms
stories.description: The US State Department has ordered a global push to bring attention to what it says are widespread efforts by Chinese companies, including AI startup DeepSeek, to steal intellectual property from US artificial intelligence labs, according to a diplomatic cable seen by Reuters. T...

stories.title: At UN panel organised by Pakistan, experts warn of terror groups exploiting AI, crypto
stories.description: UNITED NATIONS: Terrorist groups are increasingly exploiting artificial intelligence, encrypted platforms and digital currencies, transforming the global threat into a more decentralized and harder-to-detect phenomenon. These trends were discussed at a panel organised by the Perm...

stories.title: Macron leaves future open as political curtain nears
stories.description: NICOSIA: With just one year left in his second five-year term, French President Emmanuel Macron has said he will quit politics next year, leaving observers and supporters guessing about his next moves. “I wasn’t in politics before and I’m not going to be after,” Macron said on Th...

stories.title: Hezbollah says ceasefire ‘meaningless’ as fighting continues
stories.description: BEIRUT: Lebanon’s Hezbollah said a US-mediated ceasefire in the war with Israel was meaningless a day after it was extended for three weeks, as Lebanese authorities reported two people killed by an Israeli strike and Hezbollah downed an Israeli drone. Meanwhile, the Israeli milit...

stories.title: Pakistan among top 10 countries facing acute food crisis
stories.description: Source: The 2026 Global Report on Food Crises • Multi-agency report says over 9m Pakistanis face ‘crisis’ conditions; another 1.7 million in the more severe ‘emergency’ category• Notes devastating monsoon rains, severe flooding wiped out crucial cropland, livelihoods• Warns globa...

stories.title: US has exhausted billions of dollars worth of weapons stockpile in war on Iran: NYT report
stories.description: The United States has drained much of its weapons stockpile, totalling up to billions of dollars, in its war on Iran, according to a New York Times investigation. The weapons, as per the NYT, included over 1,200 Patriot interceptor missiles — each worth more than $4 million. Acco...

stories.title: Trump says cancelled envoys' Pakistan trip, but Iran war not resuming
stories.description: Iran’s Foreign Ministry said Mr. Araghchi had left Islamabad and arrived in Muscat on Saturday (April 25) for meetings with Omani officials

stories.title: U.S. Navy intercepts Iran-linked vessel in Arabian Sea
stories.description: The U.S. Department of Treasury sanctioned M/V Sevan and 18 other “shadow fleet” vessels for transporting billions in Iranian energy products to foreign markets

stories.title: U.S. sanctions waiver on Chabahar port ends on April 26, could signal end of 23-year-old connectivity project
stories.description: Officials say they are discussing a possible transfer of IPGL-subsidiary’s stake to local Iranian company temporarily to avoid sanctions

stories.title: Israel-Iran war updates: Trump cancels U.S. envoys’ visit to Pakistan for talks; says 'does not mean restart of war'
stories.description: Iran’s Foreign Minister leaves Islamabad without meeting U.S. envoys, say Pakistan officials; Iran resumes commercial flights from Tehran’s international airport for the first time since the conflict with the U.S. and Israel began two months ago

stories.title: Six killed in Israeli strikes on south Lebanon: Health Ministry
stories.description: identified two projectiles launched from Lebanon, decrying “a blatant violation of the ceasefire understandings” by Hezbollah and later intercepted another “suspicious aerial target”

stories.title: What rules govern international waters?
stories.description: Can Iran legally restrict transit through the Strait of Hormuz? Is the U.S. blockade of the strait lawful? What is the legal position on interceptions? And in cases such as this, which organisation steps in and how? What can happen next?

stories.title: Iran says US must stop threats for talks to resume — newspaper
stories.description: Sources told The Wall Street Journal that if Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi deems a deal with Washington possible, the parties could meet in the coming days

stories.title: US actions against Iran undermine trust in Washington — president
stories.description: Masoud Pezeshkian also expressed concern that the negotiations "could be a smokescreen" for a new attack on Iran

stories.title: US redirects 37 vessels since start of naval blockade of Iran — Pentagon
stories.description: CENTCOM reports that the servicemen "continue to enforce US sanctions"

stories.title: Israel attacks Hezbollah targets in southern Lebanon — army
stories.description: The Israel Defense Forces reported its attacks on military infrastructure sites
